steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage draws a contrast: non-Indigenous museums use Eurocentric strategies and anticipate mainly non-Indigenous audiences, while Indigenous centers serve the tribe's own citizens. The correct choice must state the key distinguishing feature of non-Indigenous museums.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The museums typically feature fewer artifacts in their exhibits.: cites a detail not stated in the passage as the point of contrast. ✗
  • Choice B — The museums are often somewhat smaller in size.: describes an attribute not mentioned as the distinguishing factor. ✗
  • Choice C — The museums are largely aimed at non-Indigenous audiences.: directly matches the passage's explicit claim that non-Indigenous museums "anticipate mainly non-Indigenous audiences". ✓
  • Choice D — The museums focus on tribal history as well as tribal culture.: introduces a detail absent from the passage. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
